title: Mastering Minecraft Commands
description: Learn how to create powerful commands in your Minecraft plugin with arguments, permissions, tab completion, and more.

lessons.title: Command Basics
lessons.content:
# Creating Your First Command

## Step 1: Register in plugin.yml
```yml
commands:
  hello:
    description: Says hello
```

## Step 2: Command Class
```java
public class HelloCommand implements CommandExecutor {
    @Override
    public boolean onCommand(CommandSender sender, Command command, String label, String[] args) {
        sender.sendMessage("Hello, world!");
        return true;
    }
}
```

## Step 3: Register in Main Class
```java
getCommand("hello").setExecutor(new HelloCommand());
```

## Logic
- Player types command
- Server finds command
- Executes onCommand()

## Test
Run `/hello` in game.

lessons.title: Command Sender & Player Checks
Using Arguments
Permissions
lessons.content:
# Permissions

## plugin.yml
```yml
commands:
  hello:
    permission: myplugin.hello
```

## Java Check
```java
if (!sender.hasPermission("myplugin.hello")) {
    sender.sendMessage("No permission!");
    return true;
}
```

## Logic
- Check before running command

## Test
Try command without permission.

lessons.title: Tab Completion
Advanced Command Logic
Testing & Debugging Commands
lessons.content:
# Debugging

## Example
```java
getLogger().info("Command executed");
```

## Common Errors
- Command not in plugin.yml
- Not registered
- Wrong spelling

## Testing Steps
1. Run command
2. Check console
3. Add logs

## Edge Case
Command returns false → shows usage message
